title: Roku Wireless Connection Lost
shortDescription: The TV cannot connect to the wireless network or has lost its Wi-Fi connection.
fullDescription:
Error 014 on TCL Roku TVs indicates that the TV can see your wireless network but cannot establish a connection. This is one of the most common Roku errors and specifically points to a Wi-Fi authentication or connection issue.

The error typically appears during initial setup or after the router settings have been changed. It can also occur when the router firmware is updated or when the Wi-Fi password is changed without updating it on the TV.

In most cases, re-entering the Wi-Fi password or restarting the router resolves the issue. The error code may be followed by additional digits (e.g., 014.30, 014.40) that provide more specific diagnostic information.

causes.cause: Router settings changed or firmware updated

symptoms: Error 014 displayed on screen
Unable to connect to wireless network
TV sees the network but fails to join
Connection drops during setup

fixSteps.title: Re-enter Wi-Fi password
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Wireless and select your network. Carefully re-enter the password, paying attention to uppercase and special characters.

fixSteps.title: Restart router and TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug both the router and TV for 60 seconds. Plug in the router first, wait 2 minutes for it to fully boot, then plug in the TV.

fixSteps.title: Move TV closer to router
fixSteps.instruction: If possible, reduce the distance between the TV and router. Remove any obstacles like walls or large metal objects blocking the signal path.

fixSteps.title: Check router settings
fixSteps.instruction: Log into your router's admin panel and ensure MAC address filtering is disabled or your TV's MAC address is added to the allowed list. Find the TV's MAC address in Settings > Network > About.

fixSteps.title: Reset network settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > System > Advanced System Settings > Network Connection Reset. This clears all stored network data and lets you start fresh.

whenToCallPro: If the TV cannot connect to any Wi-Fi network after resetting network settings and testing with a mobile hotspot, the Wi-Fi module may be defective.

faq.q: What does error 014 mean on my TCL Roku TV?
faq.a: Error 014 means the TV can detect your Wi-Fi network but cannot connect to it. The most common cause is an incorrect password.

faq.q: What do the extra digits after 014 mean?
faq.a: Sub-codes like 014.30 indicate the specific point where the connection failed. 014.30 means the TV could not get an IP address from the router.

title: Roku Internet Connection Failed
shortDescription: The TV is connected to the router but cannot reach the internet.
fullDescription:
Error 016 on TCL Roku TVs means the TV has successfully connected to your local network (router) but cannot access the internet. This indicates the problem is with the internet connection itself rather than the Wi-Fi link.

Common causes include ISP outages, DNS resolution failures, or router configuration issues that prevent internet traffic from reaching the TV. The TV can communicate with the router but cannot reach Roku's servers.

This error is different from 014 (which is a Wi-Fi connection problem) because the local network connection is working fine.

causes.cause: Router connected but no internet (WAN down)

symptoms: Error 016 on screen
Wi-Fi connected but apps won't load
Network test shows local connection OK but internet fails
Other devices also have no internet

fixSteps.title: Check other devices
fixSteps.instruction: Test internet on your phone or computer connected to the same network. If they also have no internet, the issue is with your ISP or router.

fixSteps.title: Restart modem and router
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug your modem (and router if separate) for 60 seconds. Plug in the modem first, wait 2 minutes, then plug in the router.

fixSteps.title: Change DNS settings
fixSteps.instruction: On the TV, go to Settings > Network > Set up connection > Advanced and set DNS to 8.8.8.8 (Google) or 1.1.1.1 (Cloudflare).

fixSteps.title: Check router firewall
fixSteps.instruction: Access your router settings and ensure it is not blocking ports used by Roku (TCP 80, 443, and UDP 5353). Disable any content filtering temporarily.

fixSteps.title: Contact ISP
fixSteps.instruction: If other devices also have no internet, contact your Internet Service Provider to check for outages in your area.

whenToCallPro: If only the TV has no internet while all other devices work fine, and DNS changes don't help, the TV's network adapter may have a configuration issue requiring factory reset.

faq.q: What is the difference between error 014 and 016?
faq.a: Error 014 means the TV cannot connect to your Wi-Fi at all. Error 016 means it connected to Wi-Fi successfully but cannot reach the internet.

faq.q: Why does my TCL TV have error 016 when my phone works fine?
faq.a: Your router may be blocking certain ports the TV needs. Try changing DNS settings on the TV or check your router's firewall settings.

title: Frequent Wi-Fi Disconnections
shortDescription: The TV repeatedly disconnects from the Wi-Fi network during use.
fullDescription:
Frequent Wi-Fi disconnections on TCL TVs cause streaming interruptions, buffering, and app crashes. The TV may show it is connected one moment and disconnected the next, often without any change to the network environment.

TCL TVs can be sensitive to certain router configurations, especially with band steering (which automatically switches between 2.4GHz and 5GHz). The TV may struggle to maintain a connection when the router keeps pushing it between bands.

The issue can also be caused by IP address conflicts, DHCP lease expiration, or interference from other wireless devices in the area.

causes.cause: Router band steering causing frequent switching

symptoms: Streaming stops and shows connection error
Wi-Fi status changes between connected and disconnected
Apps require frequent reloading
Network connection test fails intermittently

fixSteps.title: Separate Wi-Fi bands
fixSteps.instruction: In your router settings, create separate SSIDs for 2.4GHz and 5GHz bands. Connect the TV to the 5GHz network if close to the router, or 2.4GHz if far away.

fixSteps.title: Assign static IP
fixSteps.instruction: In router settings, assign a static IP address to the TV using its MAC address. This prevents IP conflicts and DHCP issues.

fixSteps.title: Change Wi-Fi channel
fixSteps.instruction: In router settings, switch from auto channel selection to a specific channel (1, 6, or 11 for 2.4GHz) to avoid interference from neighboring networks.

fixSteps.title: Use Ethernet connection
fixSteps.instruction: For the most reliable connection, connect the TV directly to the router using an Ethernet cable.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset network
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > System > Advanced System Settings > Network Connection Reset to clear all network settings and reconnect fresh.

whenToCallPro: If disconnections persist with an Ethernet cable connected, the TV's network hardware may be defective.
videoSearchQuery: TCL TV WiFi keeps disconnecting fix

faq.q: Why does my TCL TV keep losing Wi-Fi?
faq.a: The most common cause is router band steering. Separate your 2.4GHz and 5GHz networks and connect the TV to one specific band.

faq.q: Does TCL TV have an Ethernet port?
faq.a: Most TCL TVs have a built-in Ethernet port on the back panel. Using a wired connection eliminates all Wi-Fi related issues.

title: HDMI No Signal Detected
shortDescription: The TV shows 'No Signal' when an HDMI device is connected.
fullDescription:
The No Signal message on a TCL TV appears when the selected HDMI input is not receiving data from a connected device. This is a very common issue that is usually resolved without any repair.

The problem is most frequently caused by selecting the wrong input, a loose cable, or an HDMI handshake failure between the TV and the source device. For 4K content, the HDMI input mode may need to be changed to support the higher bandwidth.

TCL Roku TVs label their inputs with detected device names, which can sometimes cause confusion about which physical port is being used.

causes.cause: HDMI port set to wrong mode for 4K device

symptoms: No Signal message on screen
Black screen after selecting HDMI input
TV auto-input detection does not find device
Signal appears briefly then disappears

fixSteps.title: Select correct input
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Home button, scroll to the correct HDMI input tile on the Roku home screen. Check which physical port number the cable is plugged into.

fixSteps.title: Reseat HDMI cable
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the HDMI cable from both ends and plug it back in firmly. Try a different HDMI port on the TV.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle devices
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off both the TV and the connected device. Unplug both for 30 seconds. Turn on the source device first, then the TV.

fixSteps.title: Enable HDMI mode for 4K
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Display & Sound > HDMI mode and set the port to HDMI 2.0 for 4K devices. This enables full bandwidth for 4K HDR content.

fixSteps.title: Try a different cable
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the HDMI cable with a certified High Speed or Ultra High Speed HDMI cable, especially for 4K or gaming devices.

whenToCallPro: If no HDMI port works with multiple cables and devices, the TV's HDMI board may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: TCL TV HDMI no signal fix

faq.q: Why does my TCL TV say no signal on HDMI?
faq.a: The most common cause is selecting the wrong input. Check which HDMI port number your cable is plugged into and select that input on the home screen.

faq.q: How do I enable 4K on my TCL TV HDMI port?
faq.a: Go to Settings > Display & Sound > HDMI mode and set the relevant port to HDMI 2.0. This enables 4K HDR pass-through.

title: HDMI ARC/eARC Audio Not Working
shortDescription: Audio is not being sent to a soundbar or receiver through the HDMI ARC port.
fullDescription:
HDMI ARC issues on TCL TVs prevent audio from being sent to connected soundbars or AV receivers. The TV displays the picture normally but the external audio device remains silent or produces intermittent sound.

TCL Roku TVs require specific settings for ARC to work: CEC must be enabled (called '1 Touch Play' and 'System Audio Control' on Roku), and the correct audio output format must be selected. The ARC port on TCL TVs is typically HDMI 3 (marked with ARC).

Audio format mismatches are a frequent issue, where the TV tries to send a surround sound format that the soundbar does not support.

causes.cause: Wrong HDMI port used (not ARC port)

symptoms: No sound from soundbar through ARC
Audio plays from TV speakers instead of soundbar
Sound cuts in and out through ARC
Soundbar shows connected but no audio
Audio delay or lip-sync issues

fixSteps.title: Verify ARC port
fixSteps.instruction: Connect the soundbar to the HDMI port labeled ARC (usually HDMI 3 on TCL TVs). The soundbar's HDMI output port should be used, not an HDMI input.

fixSteps.title: Enable System Audio Control
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > System > Control Other Devices (CEC) and enable System Audio Control and 1 Touch Play.

fixSteps.title: Set audio output format
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Audio > S/PDIF and ARC and set it to PCM-Stereo if the soundbar is basic, or Auto for soundbars that support surround sound.

fixSteps.title: Disable TV speakers
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Audio > TV Speakers and set to Off when using an external audio device through ARC.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle both devices
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug both the TV and soundbar for 60 seconds. Plug in and turn on the soundbar first, then the TV.

whenToCallPro: If ARC doesn't work after verifying all settings and cables, the TV's ARC port or CEC controller may be defective.
videoSearchQuery: TCL TV HDMI ARC not working fix

faq.q: Which HDMI port is ARC on TCL TV?
faq.a: On most TCL TVs, HDMI 3 is the ARC port. Look for the 'ARC' label printed near the port on the back of the TV.

faq.q: Why does ARC audio cut in and out?
faq.a: Intermittent ARC audio is usually caused by an audio format mismatch. Set the S/PDIF and ARC output to PCM-Stereo for the most compatible format.

title: Software or Firmware Update Failed
shortDescription: A system software or Roku platform update could not be installed.
fullDescription:
Update failures on TCL TVs can affect either the TV's firmware or the Roku/Google TV operating system. The TV may display an error message, get stuck during the update process, or revert to the previous version.

TCL Roku TVs receive updates from both TCL (hardware firmware) and Roku (platform software). Either update can fail due to network interruptions, insufficient storage, or server issues. The TV normally checks for updates automatically and installs them during standby.

Forcing a manual update or using a USB drive to install firmware can bypass download-related failures.
symptoms: Update failed error message
TV restarts during update
Progress bar freezes during update
TV stuck on update screen

fixSteps.title: Retry the update
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > System > System Update > Check Now. The update may succeed on a second attempt if the server was temporarily busy.

fixSteps.title: Use a wired connection
fixSteps.instruction: Connect an Ethernet cable for a more stable connection during the update download and installation.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset then update
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > System > Advanced System Settings > Factory Reset. After resetting, the TV will download the latest software during setup.

fixSteps.title: USB firmware update
fixSteps.instruction: Visit TCL's support website, download the firmware for your exact model to a USB drive, and follow the TV's USB update procedure.

fixSteps.title: Contact TCL support
fixSteps.instruction: If the TV is stuck in a boot loop after a failed update, contact TCL support for recovery options.

whenToCallPro: If the TV is stuck on the update screen and will not boot, USB firmware recovery or professional service may be needed.
videoSearchQuery: TCL TV software update failed fix

faq.q: How do I force a software update on TCL TV?
faq.a: Go to Settings > System > System Update > Check Now. For Roku TVs, you can also try pressing Home 5 times, then Up, Rewind, Rewind, Fast Forward, Fast Forward.

faq.q: Can I update my TCL TV without internet?
faq.a: Yes, download the firmware from TCL's support website to a USB drive and install it manually through the TV's USB port.

title: No Audio from TV Speakers
shortDescription: The TV produces no sound while the picture displays normally.
fullDescription:
When a TCL TV has no audio, the picture appears normal but there is complete silence from the built-in speakers. This can happen on all inputs or only on specific apps or channels.

The most common cause is the audio being routed to an external device that is disconnected, or the TV being muted. Audio settings may have been changed accidentally, especially if someone pressed the wrong button on the remote.

If audio works on some apps but not others, the issue may be with the specific app's audio settings or an audio codec that the TV cannot decode.

causes.cause: TV volume muted or set to zero
Audio output set to external device

symptoms: No sound from TV speakers
Volume indicator shows but no audio
Sound works on some channels but not others
Sound works through headphone jack but not speakers

fixSteps.title: Check mute and volume
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Mute button on the remote to unmute. Press Volume Up to increase the volume. Check that the volume indicator appears on screen.

fixSteps.title: Check audio settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Audio > Audio Mode and ensure TV Speakers is selected. If it shows ARC or Headphones, switch it back.

fixSteps.title: Change audio format
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Audio > S/PDIF and ARC and change it to PCM-Stereo. Some content uses audio formats the TV cannot process.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV for 60 seconds, press the power button for 30 seconds, then plug it back in.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset audio
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Audio and select Reset Audio Settings to restore all audio settings to their defaults.

whenToCallPro: If audio works through the headphone jack or ARC but not through built-in speakers, the speakers or internal amplifier may need replacement.

faq.q: Why is there no sound on my TCL TV?
faq.a: Check that the TV is not muted and that audio output is set to TV Speakers in the Audio settings menu.

faq.q: Why does sound work on some apps but not others?
faq.a: Some apps output audio in formats like Dolby Digital that may not work if your audio settings are incorrect. Change S/PDIF output to PCM-Stereo.

title: Audio and Video Out of Sync
shortDescription: The audio is noticeably delayed or ahead of the video content.
fullDescription:
Audio delay (lip sync issues) on TCL TVs is when the sound does not match the movement of people's lips on screen. The audio can be either behind or ahead of the video.

This problem is particularly common when using external soundbars through HDMI ARC or Bluetooth, as the audio signal takes additional processing time to reach the external speaker. It can also occur when the TV's audio processing features add latency.

Different streaming apps may have different amounts of delay depending on how they handle audio decoding. The issue is usually fixable through the TV's audio sync settings.

causes.cause: External speaker processing delay via ARC or Bluetooth

symptoms: Audio noticeably behind video
Lip movements do not match spoken words
Audio delay worse with certain apps
Delay only when using external speakers

fixSteps.title: Adjust lip sync setting
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Audio > Lip Sync and adjust the slider until audio matches the video. Start with small adjustments.

fixSteps.title: Change audio output format
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Audio > S/PDIF and ARC and try different settings. PCM-Stereo typically has the lowest latency.

fixSteps.title: Disable audio processing
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off any audio enhancement features like Virtual Surround, Dialog Enhancement, or Volume Leveling that add processing delay.

fixSteps.title: Use HDMI ARC instead of Bluetooth
fixSteps.instruction: If using a Bluetooth speaker, switch to a wired HDMI ARC connection. Bluetooth adds significant latency compared to HDMI.

fixSteps.title: Enable Game Mode
fixSteps.instruction: For the lowest possible latency, go to Settings > Display & Sound > Picture Mode and select Game. This disables extra processing that adds delay.

whenToCallPro: Audio delay is almost always fixable through settings. Professional help is rarely needed unless there is a hardware defect in the audio processing chip.
videoSearchQuery: TCL TV audio delay lip sync fix

faq.q: Why is the audio delayed on my TCL TV?
faq.a: Audio delay is most commonly caused by external speaker processing time. Use the Lip Sync slider in Audio settings to compensate.

faq.q: Does Bluetooth cause audio delay on TV?
faq.a: Yes, Bluetooth adds 100-300ms of latency. Switch to HDMI ARC or a wired connection for real-time audio.

title: Remote Control Not Responding
shortDescription: The TCL remote control does not operate the TV or responds intermittently.
fullDescription:
A non-responsive TCL remote can use either infrared (IR) or wireless (RF/Bluetooth) technology depending on the model. Standard TCL Roku remotes are IR-based, while enhanced voice remotes use wireless connectivity.

IR remotes require a direct line of sight to the TV and can be blocked by furniture or other objects. Wireless remotes need to be paired with the TV and can lose their pairing after battery changes or TV updates.

In many cases, the issue is simply dead batteries. TCL remotes are known to drain batteries relatively quickly, especially the voice remote models.

causes.cause: IR path blocked between remote and TV

symptoms: No response from any button press
Remote works intermittently
Must point remote directly at TV from close range
Voice features not working on voice remote

fixSteps.title: Replace batteries
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the battery cover and replace with fresh AAA batteries, ensuring correct polarity. TCL voice remotes use 2 AAA batteries.

fixSteps.title: Reset the remote
fixSteps.instruction: Remove batteries, press every button once to drain residual charge, wait 30 seconds, then reinsert fresh batteries.

fixSteps.title: Re-pair wireless remote
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the pairing button inside the battery compartment for 5 seconds. The TV should show a pairing dialog.

fixSteps.title: Clear IR path
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure there are no objects between the remote and the TV's IR sensor (usually at the bottom center of the screen). Clean the IR emitter on the remote.

fixSteps.title: Use Roku app as remote
fixSteps.instruction: Download the Roku app on your smartphone and connect to the same Wi-Fi network. Use it as a temporary remote while troubleshooting.

whenToCallPro: Remote issues rarely need professional help. If the TV's IR sensor is faulty, the main board would need to be serviced.
videoSearchQuery: TCL Roku TV remote not working fix

faq.q: How do I pair my TCL Roku remote?
faq.a: Open the battery compartment and press the pairing button for 5 seconds. The TV will detect and pair the remote automatically.

faq.q: Can I use a universal remote with TCL TV?
faq.a: Yes, most universal remotes support TCL TVs. You can also use the free Roku mobile app as a fully functional remote.

title: Black Screen with or without Sound
shortDescription: The TV screen remains black while the TV appears to be powered on.
fullDescription:
A black screen on a TCL TV with the power LED lit indicates the TV is receiving power but not displaying an image. If audio can be heard, the issue is likely with the backlight or display panel rather than the main board.

The backlight failure test (shining a flashlight on the screen to see a faint image) is the key diagnostic for this issue. If a faint image is visible, the LED backlight strips have failed and need replacement.

Temporary black screen issues are commonly caused by software glitches and can be resolved with a power cycle. Persistent black screens typically indicate a hardware failure.

causes.cause: Power supply board not delivering enough voltage

symptoms: Black screen with power LED on
Sound plays but no picture
Faint image visible with flashlight
Screen goes black after startup logo

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV from the wall, hold the power button on the TV for 30 seconds, wait 5 minutes, then plug back in.

fixSteps.title: Flashlight test
fixSteps.instruction: In a dark room, turn the TV on and shine a flashlight close to the screen surface. If you can see a faint image, the backlight has failed.

fixSteps.title: Check all inputs
fixSteps.instruction: Switch between different inputs and try the Roku home screen to determine if the black screen is input-specific.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: If you can hear the TV responding to remote commands, navigate to Settings > System > Advanced > Factory Reset using the remote buttons blindly, or use the Roku app.

fixSteps.title: Seek professional repair
fixSteps.instruction: If the flashlight test confirms backlight failure, the LED strips inside the panel need to be replaced, which requires professional disassembly.

whenToCallPro: If the flashlight test reveals a faint image, professional backlight repair is needed. This involves opening the TV and replacing LED strips.

faq.q: Why is my TCL TV screen black but I hear sound?
faq.a: This usually means the LED backlight has failed. Shine a flashlight on the screen to confirm. If you see a faint image, the backlights need replacement.

faq.q: Is a black screen TV worth repairing?
faq.a: If it is a backlight issue, repair costs $100-200 which is often worth it for larger TVs. If the panel is cracked, replacement is usually not cost-effective.

title: TV Stuck on TCL or Roku Logo
shortDescription: The TV shows the TCL or Roku logo at startup and does not proceed to the home screen.
fullDescription:
When a TCL TV gets stuck on the startup logo (either the TCL logo or the Roku loading screen), the operating system cannot complete its boot process. The TV may also enter a boot loop, cycling between the logo and a black screen.

This is most commonly caused by corrupted system software, which can happen after a failed update, power loss during operation, or corrupted app data. Full internal storage can also prevent the system from booting.

A factory reset usually resolves the issue, but it requires a button combination since the TV cannot reach the settings menu.
symptoms: TCL logo displayed indefinitely
Roku loading screen never completes
TV cycles between logo and black screen
TV was working before an update or power outage

fixSteps.title: Extended power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV for 15 minutes while holding the power button for 30 seconds. This gives the system a full discharge reset.

fixSteps.title: Hardware factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the reset pinhole on the back or bottom of the TV. Press and hold the reset button with a paperclip for 15-20 seconds while the TV is plugged in.

fixSteps.title: Button combination reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV. Hold the power button on the TV. While holding, plug the TV back in and continue holding for 30 seconds. This may trigger recovery mode.

fixSteps.title: USB firmware recovery
fixSteps.instruction: Download the firmware from TCL's support site for your model, copy to a FAT32 USB drive, insert into the TV, and follow recovery prompts.

fixSteps.title: Contact TCL support
fixSteps.instruction: If no recovery method works, contact TCL support for warranty service or advanced recovery options.

whenToCallPro: If the TV remains stuck after a hardware reset and USB recovery attempt, the main board may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: TCL Roku TV stuck on logo fix

faq.q: How do I factory reset a TCL TV that won't boot?
faq.a: Use the reset pinhole on the back of the TV. Press and hold with a paperclip for 15-20 seconds while the TV is powered on.

faq.q: Will a factory reset delete my Roku channels?
faq.a: Yes, but they are tied to your Roku account. After reset, sign in and all your channels will be available to re-download.

title: Google TV Platform Error
shortDescription: The Google TV interface crashes, freezes, or displays error messages.
fullDescription:
TCL's newer TV models run Google TV (Android TV) instead of Roku. These TVs can experience platform-specific errors including app crashes, Google services errors, Play Store failures, and system UI freezes.

Google TV errors are often caused by insufficient cache storage, outdated Google Play Services, or app conflicts. The platform requires a Google account to function fully, and account authentication issues can also trigger errors.

Clearing the cache for Google Play Services and the system launcher are the most effective first steps for resolving platform-wide errors.

causes.cause: Google Play Services outdated or crashed

symptoms: Apps crash or fail to open
Google TV home screen freezes
Play Store shows error messages
System keeps restarting
Cannot sign into Google account

fixSteps.title: Clear Google Play Services cache
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Apps > See All Apps > Google Play Services > Storage & Cache > Clear Cache.

fixSteps.title: Clear system launcher cache
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Apps > See All Apps > show system apps > Google TV Home > Storage & Cache > Clear Cache.

fixSteps.title: Check for system updates
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > System > About > System Update. Install any available updates for the TV and Google TV platform.

fixSteps.title: Re-add Google account
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Accounts & Sign In, remove your Google account, restart the TV, and add the account again.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > System > About > Reset > Factory Data Reset. This erases all data and reinstalls the Google TV platform fresh.

whenToCallPro: Google TV errors are software-based and almost always fixable without professional help. Contact TCL support only if issues persist after a factory reset.

faq.q: What is the difference between TCL Roku TV and Google TV?
faq.a: TCL makes TVs with both platforms. Roku TVs use the Roku operating system, while newer models use Google TV (Android). The troubleshooting steps differ for each.

faq.q: Can I switch from Google TV to Roku?
faq.a: No, the operating system is built into the hardware. You cannot switch platforms, but you can connect an external Roku streaming device.

title: Screen Flickering or Flashing
shortDescription: The TV display flickers, flashes, or has unstable brightness.
fullDescription:
Screen flickering on TCL TVs can appear as rapid brightness changes, visible strobing, or intermittent flashing. The issue can affect the whole screen or only portions of it.

TCL TVs with local dimming (found in 5-Series and above) are more prone to visible flickering as the dimming zones adjust. Aggressive local dimming settings combined with high contrast content can cause noticeable flicker.

Flickering can also be caused by a mismatch between the content refresh rate and the TV's display refresh rate, a failing LED driver, or loose internal connections.
symptoms: Screen brightness fluctuates rapidly
Visible strobing or flashing
Flickering worse in dark scenes
Flickering on specific inputs or apps

fixSteps.title: Adjust local dimming
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Display & Sound > Picture Settings > Advanced > Local Dimming and set it to Low or Off.

fixSteps.title: Disable dynamic backlight
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Display & Sound > Picture Settings > Advanced > Dynamic Backlight and turn it off.

fixSteps.title: Change picture mode
fixSteps.instruction: Switch to a different picture mode such as Movie or Normal. Some modes like Vivid use aggressive processing that can cause flicker.

fixSteps.title: Disable motion smoothing
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Advanced Picture Settings and turn off Action Smoothing/Motion Enhancement. This feature can cause flickering on some content.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Check for firmware updates that may address flickering issues specific to your model.

whenToCallPro: If flickering persists after adjusting all picture settings and updating firmware, the LED driver or internal connections may need professional inspection.

faq.q: Why does my TCL TV flicker in dark scenes?
faq.a: This is typically caused by the local dimming feature. Set Local Dimming to Low or Off in the picture settings to reduce or eliminate flickering.

faq.q: Is screen flickering a sign of a bad TV?
faq.a: Not necessarily. Most flickering on TCL TVs is caused by picture processing settings and can be resolved without repair.

title: Roku No Wireless Network Found
shortDescription: The TV cannot detect any wireless networks during setup or connection.
fullDescription:
Error 001 on TCL Roku TVs indicates that the TV's wireless adapter cannot find any Wi-Fi networks. Unlike error 014 (which means a specific network was found but connection failed), error 001 means no networks are detected at all.

This can be caused by the Wi-Fi adapter being disabled, a hardware fault with the wireless module, or being completely out of range of any wireless network. In some cases, a software glitch can temporarily disable the Wi-Fi scanner.

If the TV previously connected to Wi-Fi and suddenly shows this error, a software issue is the most likely cause. If it is a new TV that has never connected, check that you are within range of your router.

causes.cause: TV too far from any wireless network

symptoms: Error 001 displayed during network setup
No wireless networks shown in list
Wi-Fi scan finds nothing
Previously connected TV suddenly cannot find networks

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV for 60 seconds, hold the power button for 30 seconds, then plug it back in and try scanning for networks again.

fixSteps.title: Restart your router
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the router for 30 seconds and plug it back in. Wait 2 minutes for it to fully boot before scanning on the TV.

fixSteps.title: Move TV closer to router
fixSteps.instruction: Temporarily move the TV closer to the router or bring a mobile hotspot next to the TV to test if the Wi-Fi module is working.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: Use the reset pinhole on the back of the TV to perform a factory reset, which reinitializes the Wi-Fi module.

fixSteps.title: Use Ethernet as alternative
fixSteps.instruction: Connect an Ethernet cable to bypass the Wi-Fi issue entirely. This also confirms whether the network hardware is partially functional.

whenToCallPro: If the TV cannot detect any network even when placed next to a router and after a factory reset, the Wi-Fi module is likely defective and needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: TCL Roku TV error 001 no network found fix

faq.q: Why can't my TCL TV find any Wi-Fi networks?
faq.a: Error 001 means the Wi-Fi scanner found nothing. Try power cycling the TV first. If that does not help, test with a mobile hotspot held next to the TV.

faq.q: Can a TCL TV work without Wi-Fi?
faq.a: Yes, you can use HDMI inputs for cable boxes, gaming consoles, and streaming devices. You can also connect via Ethernet cable for internet access.

title: TV Will Not Power On
shortDescription: The TV does not respond to the power button and shows no signs of life.
fullDescription:
When a TCL TV will not turn on, there is no LED indicator light, no sound, and no response to the remote or the physical power button on the TV. This indicates a complete power failure rather than a black screen issue.

The most common cause is a simple power delivery issue such as a tripped circuit breaker, faulty power strip, or loose power cord. TCL TVs have a known issue where the power button location can be difficult to find, leading users to think the TV is unresponsive.

If the power outlet and cable are confirmed working, the issue is likely a failed power supply board inside the TV, which can fail due to power surges or component aging.

causes.cause: Power outlet dead or breaker tripped

symptoms: No LED indicator light
No response to remote or TV buttons
No sound or image at all
TV worked previously and suddenly stopped

fixSteps.title: Check power outlet
fixSteps.instruction: Test the outlet by plugging in another device. Check your circuit breaker panel for any tripped breakers.

fixSteps.title: Inspect power cord
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the power cord is firmly plugged into both the TV and wall outlet. Check for any visible damage to the cord.

fixSteps.title: Bypass power strip
fixSteps.instruction: If using a power strip or surge protector, plug the TV directly into a wall outlet to rule out a faulty power strip.

fixSteps.title: Hard reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV, hold the power button on the TV for 60 seconds, wait 10 minutes, then plug back in and try to turn on.

fixSteps.title: Inspect power supply board
fixSteps.instruction: For advanced users, remove the back panel and visually inspect the power supply board for bulging capacitors or burn marks.

whenToCallPro: If the TV has no power after verifying the outlet, cord, and performing a hard reset, the power supply board likely needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: TCL TV won't turn on fix

faq.q: Where is the power button on a TCL TV?
faq.a: The power button is usually located on the bottom center or bottom right of the TV frame. Some models have it on the back panel.

faq.q: How much does it cost to fix a TCL TV that won't turn on?
faq.a: A replacement power supply board costs $20-40. Professional repair including labor typically runs $75-150.
